title: calceolaria harlingii
intro: Calceolaria harlingii, also known as the slipper flower, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Calceolariaceae family. It is native to the high-altitude Andean regions of South America, where it has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Calceolaria harlingii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
